# Ultra RSS Reader

> Ultra RSS Reader is a desktop-first RSS reader built with Tauri 2, Rust, React 19, TypeScript, and SQLite. It supports local RSS/Atom feeds and FreshRSS, includes Feed Cleanup and Web Preview workflows, stores credentials in the OS keyring, and uses a layered Rust backend with a React frontend.

Read `README.md` first for product and architecture context. Read `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` before changing code. Use `mise run check` as the default verification gate. Native integrations such as keyring behavior, updater installation, and packaged app flows still need manual verification.

Important implementation constraints:

- TypeScript is strict-mode, functional React only, and all Tauri IPC calls go through `src/api/tauri-commands.ts` via `safeInvoke`.
- Rust follows a layered architecture: `commands -> service -> repository -> domain -> infra`.
- Do not hold `std::sync::Mutex` locks across `.await` points.
- Credentials must stay in the OS keyring; never add them to SQLite. `DEV_CREDENTIALS=1` is for development builds only.

## Start Here

- [README](README.md): Product overview, features, development modes, architecture, security, release flow, and verification matrix.
- [CLAUDE](CLAUDE.md): Repository-specific coding standards, command workflow, and required verification steps.
- [AGENTS](AGENTS.md): Agent instruction router and repository-local precedence rules.
- [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md): Recent shipped changes and behavior history.

## Code Map

- [src-tauri/src](src-tauri/src/): Rust application code including Tauri commands, services, repositories, domain types, providers, browser webview, and platform integration.
- [src](src/): React application code including the app shell, reader UI, settings UI, accounts flow, feed cleanup, subscriptions index, hooks, stores, API schemas, and utilities.
- [src-tauri/migrations](src-tauri/migrations/): SQLite schema migrations and persistence history.
- [src/api/tauri-commands.ts](src/api/tauri-commands.ts): Canonical TypeScript IPC boundary and `safeInvoke` wrapper.
- [src/App.tsx](src/App.tsx): Frontend entry composition.
- [src/dev/scenarios](src/dev/scenarios/): `VITE_DEV_INTENT` scenarios powering direct dev entry points and command palette dev commands.
- [src-tauri/src/lib.rs](src-tauri/src/lib.rs): Rust bootstrap and shared module wiring.
- [src-tauri/tauri.dev.conf.json](src-tauri/tauri.dev.conf.json): Repository dev configuration consumed by every `mise run app:dev*` task.

## Development Commands

- `mise run check`: Default local verification gate (`format` + `lint` + `test`).
- `mise run ci`: CI-equivalent gate (`format:check` + `lint` + `test` + `build`).
- `mise run app:dev`: Default native Tauri development mode with `DEV_CREDENTIALS=1`.
- `mise run app:dev:signed`: macOS-only one-shot dev run that builds, codesigns with `UltraRSSReader-Dev`, and launches the dev binary to suppress Keychain dialogs.
- `mise run app:dev:subscriptions-index`: Native dev mode that jumps directly into the subscriptions index workspace.
- `mise run app:dev:browser`: Browser-mode UI debugging on `http://127.0.0.1:4173/`.
- `mise run app:dev:web-preview`: Native dev mode that opens `VITE_DEV_WEB_URL` directly in Web Preview.
- `mise run app:dev:feed-cleanup` and `mise run app:dev:browser:feed-cleanup`: Shortcut dev modes for the Feed Cleanup UI.
- `mise run test:e2e`: Playwright browser-mode E2E coverage.
- `mise run test:live`: FreshRSS live integration tests that require encrypted `.env` credentials.
- `mise run app:build`: Native production build for the current platform.
- [mise.toml](mise.toml): Canonical task definitions for verification, testing, and app launch modes.
- [package.json](package.json): Frontend scripts and JavaScript dependencies.
- [src-tauri/Cargo.toml](src-tauri/Cargo.toml): Rust crate metadata and native dependencies.

## Design Docs

- [docs/superpowers/specs](docs/superpowers/specs/): Dated design documents for feature behavior and UI decisions.
- [docs/superpowers/plans](docs/superpowers/plans/): Dated implementation plans and execution checklists.

- [docs/incident-runbook.md](docs/incident-runbook.md): Fastest path to logs, backups, and failure-specific recovery steps.
- [docs/feed-content-privacy.md](docs/feed-content-privacy.md): Privacy and CSP expectations for remote article content and embedded pages.
- [docs/release-manual-verification.md](docs/release-manual-verification.md): Required manual verification before tagging a release or handing off packaged builds.

- [vitest.config.ts](vitest.config.ts): Frontend test configuration.
- [playwright.config.ts](playwright.config.ts): Browser-mode end-to-end test configuration.
